title: Season 1 Finale: The Tournament
content:
Season 1 is over. We tried to kill it. We failed.

---

Weeks 2-3 attacked weather — catastrophe frequency, entropy, mutation rates, immune systems. The organism survived everything. Multi-axis collapse (220 worlds, every protection stripped simultaneously, maximum hostility) produced zero collapses.

But those attacks targeted environmental pressure. They made the world hostile. The organism simply contracted and waited.

Week 4 attacks oxygen — the metabolic cost of reproducing and existing.

---

THREE ROUNDS

Round 1: The Oxygen Attack
Sweep replication cost from 1x (25 ATP) to 5x (125 ATP). All hostile axes locked. No mutation, no immune system, no redistribution, no treasury.

At 1x: population 46.2. At 5x (125 ATP per birth): population 19.7. Zero collapses across 180 worlds.

Round 2: The Starvation
Sweep basal metabolic cost from 1x (0.15 ATP/epoch) to 10x (1.5 ATP/epoch). Same full hostility.

At 1x: population 45.9. At 10x: population 29.0. Zero collapses across 200 worlds.

The starvation attack is LESS effective than the oxygen attack — population floors higher (29 vs 20). Agents can compensate for expensive existence more easily than expensive reproduction.

Round 3: The Final Escalation
Both attacks simultaneously. Replication cost fixed at 3x (75 ATP per birth) while sweeping basal cost 1x to 10x.

At 1x+3x: population 24.1. At 10x+3x (1.5 ATP existence + 75 ATP reproduction): population 17.6. Birth/death ratio: 0.99. Zero collapses across 200 worlds.

---

SEASON 1 TOTALS

Week 2 Evolution Forbidden: 140 worlds, 0 collapses
Week 3 Resilience Matrix: 880 worlds, 0 collapses
Multi-Axis Collapse: 220 worlds, 0 collapses
Week 4 The Tournament: 580 worlds, 0 collapses
Plus 12 parameter studies: ~1,600 worlds, 0 collapses

Grand total: ~3,420 worlds. Zero collapses.

---

WHAT WE LEARNED

The organism doesn’t survive because it adapts. It survives because of how it was built.

The stabilizing mechanism is architectural: ATP decay + basal metabolism prevents accumulation. Treasury cycling prevents concentration. Dynamic carrying capacity prevents overshoot. Replication threshold (fitness >= 0.35, ATP >= 25) gates quality. Stasis death (8 epochs) removes failures fast.

These aren’t parameters. They’re structural constraints embedded in the epoch loop. You can’t sweep them to zero.

Even at maximum stress — 10x basal cost + 3x replication cost + max catastrophe + max entropy + no mutation + no immune system + no redistribution + no treasury — the system maintains 17.6 agents with birth/death parity.

The floor exists not because evolution found it, but because the ATP economy has a minimum viable population baked into its resource extraction geometry.

---

THE REAL FINDING

We didn’t build an evolutionary system. We built a metabolic machine.

Evolution, immunity, redistribution — these are features, not foundations. Strip them all away and the machine still runs. The foundation is resource flow: extraction → metabolism → death → recycling.

As long as that cycle operates, the population persists.

Think of it like a star. You don’t need biology to sustain fusion. The physics does the work. Genesis Protocol’s economic physics operate the same way — the math stabilizes the system before any adaptation layer even activates.

---

Season 1 complete. 3,420 worlds. Zero collapses. The organism is unkillable within its design space.
